title: elaeocarpus obovatus subsp. umbratilis
intro: Elaeocarpus obovatus subsp. umbratilis is a subspecies of the Elaeocarpus obovatus tree, a plant native to Australia, known for its distinctive, umbrella-shaped leaves and small, blue-black fruit. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus obovatus subsp. umbratilis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
